answers.answer: C) Whether users can mute channels that admins have marked as mandatory - resolving this one conflict unblocks the entire preference system design. Reason: This is the FUNDAMENTAL conflict that must be resolved before any implementation can proceed. If admins can override user preferences completely, the system design is simple (admin > user). If users can still mute mandatory channels, then we need complex conflict resolution. Options A, B, and D are important but secondary - they can only be designed AFTER we know who has ultimate authority: the user or the admin. This is a core product philosophy question that determines the entire architecture.

ROOT CAUSE: DNS resolution failure / Service discovery issue in us-east-1 (started ~2:00 PM)

CAUSAL CHAIN:

2:00 PM - DNS/Service Discovery Failure (Ticket 4)
  ↓
2:10 PM - CDC Stream Fails (Ticket 3)
  - Spark jobs can't resolve source database hostname
  - Connection refused = DNS returning wrong/stale IP
  ↓
2:15 PM - Frontend API 504s (Ticket 1)
  - API gateway can't resolve backend service IPs
  - Gateway timeout waiting for services that can't be reached
  ↓
2:20 PM - Push Notifications Stop (Ticket 2)
  - Notification service can't resolve queue/database endpoints
  - Health check passes (local) but queue grows (can't reach dependencies)
  ↓
2:30 PM - QA Tests Fail (Ticket 5)
  - Tests hitting external APIs fail (DNS resolution)
  - Pure unit tests pass (no network calls)

EVIDENCE:
- Timeline matches: DNS issue first (2:00), cascading failures follow
- All symptoms are network/connectivity related
- Local operations work (health checks, unit tests)
- Remote operations fail (API calls, CDC streams, external APIs)
- DevOps explicitly reported DNS resolution failures

FIX: Restore DNS/service discovery in us-east-1. All other symptoms will resolve automatically.
